amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: The Snf1 kinase (yeast AMPK) senses low energy/glucose and activates alternative carbon source utilization. Under low glucose, Snf1 is activated by upstream kinases (Sak1/Elm1/Tos3) phosphorylating Thr210. Active Snf1 phosphorylates Mig1 repressor (nuclear export, derepressing SUC2/GAL genes), Adr1 activator (nuclear import, activating ADH2), and metabolic enzymes. The pathway integrates with TOR, PKA, and stress responses. Snf1 is heterotrimeric with catalytic (Snf1), scaffold (Snf4), and regulatory (Gal83/Sip1/Sip2) subunits.

name: Snf1/AMPK Energy Sensing Pathway
notes: Shows high AND gates (7) for Snf1 complex assembly. Conserved energy sensor (AMPK in humans).
scientificAccuracy: Based on extensive research on yeast Snf1/AMPK, conserved energy sensor.

sources.title: The AMP-activated protein kinase and its yeast homolog SNF1: an ancient energy sensor controlling metabolism
sources.authors: Hardie DG, Carling D, Carlson M

sources.title: Snf1-activating kinases Sak1, Elm1 and Tos3 regulate yeast carbon metabolism
